name: Sicilian Ragu
recipeIngredient: 1 Small sweet onion, peeled and minced
1/2 lb Ground pork, or 1/2 lb Plain Italian sausage (meat removed from casings and crumbled)
2 Sprigs fresh mint or 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme
Salt & freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a 3-quart non-aluminum saucepan.
Add the onion, celery, and carrot and sauté over low heat until the vegetables just begin to color. Use a slotted spoon to remove the vegetables to a plate. Set aside until needed.
2. Add the remaining tablespoon of olive oil to the pan and sauté the ground pork until it is lightly browned. Pour off and discard any excess fat from the pan. Return the vegetables to the pan.
3. Puree the tomatoes and add them to the saucepan along with the mint, parsley, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, lower the heat, and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally for 1 1/2 hours.
This sauce may be refrigerated in a tightly covered container for up to one week. It can also be frozen for up to 4 months.
